intro: The Steffensia plantaginea, also known as Steffensia, is a flowering plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to Brazil. The Steffensia plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Steffensia plantaginea include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as diterpenes and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Steffensia is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
